questions.question: What is the topic for Week 35?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-Five is PARENTING STYLES (PART 1).

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 34?
questions.explanation: Social Media Romance was the topic for Week 34.

questions.question: And the younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the portion of goods that falls to me.’ So he divided to them his ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Luke 15:12) says 'So he divided to them his livelihood.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Luke 15:12.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 35 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: And the younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the portion of goods that falls to me.’ So he divided to them his livelihood.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Luke 15:12.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Luke 15:12), what did the younger son ask his father for?
options: A. The best robe and a ring
B. The portion of goods that falls to him
C. To be made one of his hired servants
questions.explanation: The verse says 'Father, give me the portion of goods that falls to me.'

questions.question: In the introduction, 'responsiveness' is described as the extent to which parents are:
options: A. Warm and sensitive to their children
B. Strict and demanding of obedience
questions.explanation: The text states '“responsiveness” (the extent to which parents are warm and sensitive to their children)'

questions.question: How does the introduction define 'demandingness'?
options: A. The extent to which parents are warm and sensitive
B. The extent of control parents put on their children
C. How much money parents make
D. The number of chores parents give
questions.explanation: The text states '“demandingness” (the extent of control parents put on their children)'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'PERMISSIVE AND UNINVOLVED'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, permissive parents are high in responsiveness/love but low in what?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Permissive parents are high “responsiveness/love” but low in “demandingness/discipline.”'

questions.question: Which parenting style involves giving in to children's demands to make them happy and avoid conflicts, even at their own expense?
questions.explanation: The text describes this under 'Permissive' parenting.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what are some traits common under permissive parenting?
options: B. Delinquent behaviours and the inability to make the right decisions
D. Fear of becoming dependent on others
questions.explanation: The text says 'Delinquent behaviours and the inability to make the right decisions are traits common under permissive parenting.'

questions.question: Neglectful/Uninvolved parents are described as being:
options: A. High in love, high in discipline
B. High in love, low in discipline
C. Low in love, high in discipline
D. Low in responsiveness/love and low in demandingness/discipline
questions.explanation: The text says 'Neglectful/Uninvolved parents are low in “responsiveness/love” and low in “demandingness/discipline.”'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'AUTHORITARIAN AND AUTHORITATIVE'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, which parenting style is high in demandingness/discipline but low in responsiveness/love?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Authoritarian parents are high in “demandingness/discipline” but low in “responsiveness/love.”'

questions.question: Authoritarian parents use rigid parenting and harsh discipline, often justified as 'tough love'.
questions.explanation: The text says 'This rigid parenting style uses harsh discipline, justified as “tough love” (1 Samuel 20:30).'

questions.question: Which of these are common traits in children of authoritarian parents?
options: C. Low self-esteem, depression, aggression and poor social skills
D. Socially competent and can think for themselves
questions.explanation: The text states 'Low self-esteem, depression, aggression and poor social skills are common traits in these children...'

questions.question: How are authoritative parents described in terms of responsiveness and demandingness?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Authoritative parents are high in “responsiveness/love” and high in “demandingness/discipline.”'

questions.question: Children of authoritative parents tend to be self-disciplined, socially competent, and can think for themselves.
questions.explanation: The text states 'Children who have authoritative parents tend to be self-disciplined, socially competent and can think for themselves...'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Now Eli was very old; and he heard everything his sons did to all Israel, and how they lay with the women who assembled at the door of the tabernacle of meeting.'
questions.verse_text: Now Eli was very old; and he heard everything his sons did to all Israel, and how they lay with the women who assembled at the door of the tabernacle of meeting.
questions.explanation: This is verse 22 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (1 Samuel 2:23), what did Eli say to his sons when he heard of their evil dealings?
options: A. 'Why do you do such things?'
B. 'You are banished from the tabernacle.'
C. 'I will offer a sacrifice for you.'
D. 'You are no longer my sons.'
questions.explanation: Verse 23 says 'So he said to them, “Why do you do such things? For I hear of your evil dealings from all the people.”'

questions.question: According to 1 Samuel 2:24, what did Eli say his sons were making the Lord's people do?
questions.explanation: Verse 24 says 'You make the Lord’s people transgress.'

questions.question: In 1 Samuel 2:25, Eli asks, 'But if a man sins against the Lord, who will ________ for him?'
questions.explanation: Verse 25 says 'who will intercede for him?'

questions.question: According to 1 Samuel 2:25, why did Eli's sons not heed the voice of their father?
options: B. Because the Lord desired to kill them
C. Because they were older than him
questions.explanation: Verse 25 says 'Nevertheless they did not heed the voice of their father, because the Lord desired to kill them.'

questions.question: According to 1 Samuel 2:29, God rebuked Eli asking why he honored his sons more than whom?
questions.explanation: Verse 29 says 'and honor your sons more than Me...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding permissive parents who give in to demands to make their children happy and avoid conflicts (like a father giving inheritance early)?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Luke 15:12)' regarding giving in to demands.

questions.question: Permissive parenting has the potential to increase self-esteem and creativity in children.
questions.explanation: The text states 'However, it has the potential to increase self-esteem and creativity in children.'

questions.question: In an attempt to be in control, ________ parents often talk to their children without expecting feedback.
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'authoritarian parents often talk to their children without expecting feedback.'

questions.question: Which biblical characters are given as examples of children who had authoritative parents (self-disciplined, socially competent)?
options: B. Mordecai and Esther, Naomi and Ruth
questions.explanation: Outline II says '(e.g. Mordecai and Esther, Naomi and Ruth)'.

questions.question: According to the conclusion, your parenting style will greatly affect your children's lives.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states 'Understand that your parenting style will greatly affect your children’s lives.'
